title: SmartAgri Agentic RAG — System Output Schema
description: Full JSON schema defining the expected output structure of the SmartAgri Agentic RAG system. Every recommendation returned by the Planning Agent must conform to this schema.

session_id.description: Unique identifier for this processing session.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the system produced this output (UTC+7 for Vietnam).

sensor_snapshot.description: Snapshot of all sensor readings collected at the time of processing.

location.description: Geographic and administrative context of the monitored field.

elevation_m.description: Elevation above sea level in metres.

crop_info.description: Current crop type and phenological stage.

crop_type.description: Primary crop type in the monitored field.

growth_stage.description: Current phenological growth stage (BBCH scale adapted for tropical rice).

days_after_sowing.description: Number of days since the crop was sown or transplanted.

planting_date.description: Date the crop was sown or transplanted.

readings.description: Array of individual sensor readings included in this snapshot.

sensor_id.description: Unique hardware identifier of the sensor unit.

sensor_type.description: Type of environmental variable being measured.

value.description: Processed sensor reading after edge-layer filtering. Null if missing and imputation was not possible.

recorded_at.description: Exact timestamp when this reading was captured by the sensor.

status.description: Quality flag assigned by the edge processing and data quality layers.

is_imputed.description: True if the value was generated by the AI imputation model (LSTM or neighbour interpolation).

raw_value.description: Original pre-imputation value. Populated only when is_imputed is true.

imputation_method.description: Method used to impute this value, if applicable.

data_quality.description: Composite data quality assessment and R_score calculation for this snapshot.

confidence_level.description: Qualitative confidence tier derived from R_score: high(≥0.85), medium(0.65–0.84), low(0.40–0.64), unreliable(<0.40).

s_completeness.description: Fraction of expected sensor readings received in the past 1 hour. (readings_received / readings_expected)

s_consistency.description: Inter-sensor consistency score for sensors of the same type within the field zone. Calculated as 1 − (std_dev / mean) where multiple sensors exist.

s_plausibility.description: Fraction of readings that fall within their absolute physical validity bounds.

issues.description: List of data quality problems detected in this snapshot.

sensor_id.description: The sensor affected by this issue.

issue_type.description: Classification of the data quality issue.

action_taken.description: Automated remediation action applied by the system.

recommendation.description: The core output of the Planning Agent: a structured agricultural recommendation with full traceability.

status.description: Output status: 'success' = R_score meets threshold and evidence found; 'refused' = R_score < 0.40; 'escalated' = conflicting sensors requiring human verification; 'low_confidence' = recommendation issued with caveats.

primary_action.description: The principal recommended action — specific, actionable, and unambiguous.

description.description: Full natural-language recommendation in English. Must be specific and immediately actionable.
description.example: Apply 25–30 mm of irrigation within the next 6 hours. Irrigate during the early morning (05:00–07:00) or late afternoon (17:00–18:00) to minimise evaporation losses.

urgency.description: Time horizon within which the action should be taken.

timing.description: Recommended time window for the action.

secondary_actions.description: Optional follow-up or supplementary actions recommended alongside the primary action.

example.description: Re-check soil moisture 4 hours after irrigation to confirm the field has reached the target moisture level (>50%).

reasoning_chain.description: Step-by-step chain-of-thought trace from the Planning Agent. Each step documents what the agent observed, inferred, retrieved, or concluded.

action.description: Type of agent action at this step.

input.description: What the agent used as input for this step.

output.description: What the agent concluded or produced at this step.

example.output: Air temperature 38.5°C, soil moisture 18.2%, no rainfall in 24 h, water level 3 cm

example.input: Optimal thresholds for rice at heading stage
example.output: Temperature exceeds safe limit (>35°C); soil moisture below minimum (18% < 50%)

example.input: Query: irrigation rice heading stage high temperature water deficit
example.output: 5 chunks retrieved from MARD 2022, IRRI 2021, Can Tho University 2023
Evidence confirmed; R_score 0.93 exceeds high-confidence threshold (0.85)
Apply 25–30 mm irrigation immediately; prioritise early morning application

evidence.description: Knowledge chunks retrieved from the vector database that support this recommendation.

source_id.description: Unique chunk identifier in the vector database.

source_name.description: Full name of the source document.

page.description: Page number within the source document.

excerpt_summary.description: Concise paraphrase of the relevant passage (never verbatim reproduction).
excerpt_summary.example: Heading stage requires soil moisture above 50% and air temperature between 22–30°C. Water deficit lasting 48 hours during this stage reduces grain yield by 20–40%.

similarity_score.description: Cosine similarity between the retrieval query embedding and this chunk's embedding.

chunk_rank.description: Rank of this chunk in the retrieval results (1 = most relevant).

url.description: Public URL of the source document if available.

warnings.description: Caveats, limitations, or safety notices associated with this recommendation.

example.message: Inspect and clear irrigation nozzles before applying water to ensure uniform distribution.
If air temperature remains above 40°C for more than 12 hours after irrigation, escalate to a local agricultural extension officer.

metadata.description: Technical metadata about the processing session for auditing and performance monitoring.

processing_time_ms.description: Total end-to-end latency from input receipt to output generation, in milliseconds.

agent_version.description: Software version of the Agentic RAG system.

embedding_model.description: Embedding model used for query and chunk vectorisation.

retrieval_top_k.description: Number of chunks retrieved before reranking.

chunks_retrieved.description: Number of chunks returned by the vector search.

chunks_used.description: Number of chunks ultimately used in the final generation step.

knowledge_base_size.description: Total number of indexed chunks in the knowledge base at query time.

reasoning_steps.description: Total number of chain-of-thought steps taken by the agent.

self_verified.description: True if the agent performed a self-verification check before returning output.

fallback_used.description: True if the agent had to re-issue a reformulated retrieval query due to insufficient initial results.

examples._comment: Full output example — S1 Normal scenario: rice at heading stage, critical heat + drought stress

primary_action.description: Apply 25–30 mm of irrigation within the next 6 hours. Irrigate during the early morning (05:00–07:00) or late afternoon (17:00–18:00) to minimise evaporation losses and reduce canopy heat stress.

secondary_actions.description: Re-measure soil moisture 4 hours after irrigation to confirm recovery above 50%.

reasoning_chain.output: Air temp 38.5°C; soil moisture 18.2%; no rainfall in 24 h; water level 3 cm; EC normal; pH 6.1

reasoning_chain.input: Agronomic thresholds for rice at heading stage
reasoning_chain.output: CRITICAL: Temperature >35°C (heat stress); soil moisture 18% far below minimum 50% (drought stress)

reasoning_chain.input: rice heading stage irrigation high temperature water deficit mekong delta
reasoning_chain.output: 5 chunks retrieved: MARD 2022 (p.47), IRRI 2021 (p.112), Can Tho Univ 2023 (p.34), FAO 2020 (p.78), VNMHA 2022 (p.15)
MARD 2022 p.47 ranked #1 (similarity=0.91); IRRI 2021 p.112 ranked #2 (similarity=0.87)
Evidence confirmed and directly relevant; R_score 0.93 exceeds high-confidence threshold (0.85). No retry needed.
Apply 25–30 mm irrigation immediately; early morning or late afternoon preferred to minimise evaporation

evidence.source_name: Rice Cultivation Technical Guidelines — Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development (MARD), Vietnam, 2022
evidence.excerpt_summary: The heading stage is the most water-sensitive phase of rice development. Soil moisture must remain above 50% and air temperature should not exceed 35°C. A water deficit of 48 hours during this window can reduce grain yield by 20–40%.

evidence.source_name: Water Management for Irrigated Rice — IRRI Technical Bulletin, 2021
evidence.excerpt_summary: Under high-temperature conditions (>35°C) during the reproductive stage, supplemental irrigation of 25–30 mm applied in the early morning is recommended to lower canopy temperature and prevent spikelet sterility.

warnings.message: Inspect irrigation nozzles and pipelines before starting to ensure uniform water distribution across the field.
If air temperature remains above 40°C for more than 12 consecutive hours after irrigation, escalate to a local agricultural extension officer immediately.
